597→def test_create_impl_context_valid_ref_json(tmp_path: Path) -> None: 598→ """ref.json with valid fields extracts all metadata correctly.""" 599→ result, impl_path = _invoke_create_impl_context( 600→ tmp_path, 601→ "plan-test-branch", 602→ impl_context_files={ 603→ "plan.md": "# Plan\n\nContent.", 604→ "ref.json": json.dumps( 605→ { 606→ "objective_id": 42, 607→ "title": "Custom Title", 608→ "node_ids": ["node-1", "node-2"], 609→ } 610→ ), 611→ }, 612→ ) 613→ assert result["success"] is True 614→ assert result["title"] == "Custom Title" 615→ ref_data = json.loads((impl_path / "ref.json").read_text(encoding="utf-8")) 616→ assert ref_data["objective_id"] == 42 617→ assert ref_data["node_ids"] == ["node-1", "node-2"] 618→ 619→ 620→def test_planned_pr_pr_not_found_reports_error(tmp_path: Path) -> None: 621→ """When the PR doesn't exist, report an error.""" 622→ # Create a PlannedPRBackend but don't create any PR 623→ fake_github = FakeLocalGitHub() 624→ backend = PlannedPRBackend(fake_github, fake_github.issues, time=FakeTime()) 625→ 626→ fake_git = FakeGit(current_branches={tmp_path: "master"})

src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/setup_impl_from_pr.py:116:def create_impl_context_from_pr(

116→def create_impl_context_from_pr( 117→ ctx: click.Context, 118→ *, 119→ pr_number: int, 120→ cwd: Path, 121→ branch_name: str, 122→) -> dict[str, str | int | bool | None]: 123→ """Create .erk/impl-context/ folder from a GitHub PR's plan content. 124→ 125→ Fetches PR metadata, reads plan content (from committed .erk/impl-context/ 126→ files or PR body fallback), and creates the impl folder structure. 127→ 128→ Does NOT checkout branches or create worktrees — the caller must already 129→ be on the correct branch/worktree. 130→ 131→ Args: 132→ ctx: Click context (for gateway access) 133→ pr_number: PR number containing the plan 134→ cwd: Working directory (worktree root) 135→ branch_name: Branch name for impl-context scoping 136→ 137→ Returns: 138→ Dict with success status, impl_path, pr_number, pr_url, branch, 139→ and pr_title. 140→ """ 141→ repo_root = require_repo_root(ctx) 142→ github = require_github(ctx) 143→ 144→ pr_result = github.get_pr(repo_root, pr_number) 145→ if isinstance(pr_result, PRNotFound):
... [70 lines omitted] ...

All fixes are complete. Here is a summary:
**Fixed 6 files, 20 total `pr_id=` to `plan_id=` replacements inside `save_plan_ref()` calls:**
1. `/Users/schrockn/.erk/repos/erk/worktrees/erk-slot-49/tests/core/test_impl_folder.py` -- 9 occurrences (lines 498, 526, 542, 561, 649, 679, 701, 732, 758) 2. `/Users/schrockn/.erk/repos/erk/worktrees/erk-slot-49/tests/core/test_impl_issue_wt_workflow.py` -- 4 occurrences (lines 35, 63, 87, 135) 3. `/Users/schrockn/.erk/repos/erk/worktrees/erk-slot-49/tests/commands/pr/test_list.py` -- 3 occurrences (lines 412, 518, 534) 4. `/Users/schrockn/.erk/repos/erk/worktrees/erk-slot-49/tests/unit/status/test_impl_collector.py` -- 1 occurrence (line 60) 5. `/Users/schrockn/.erk/repos/erk/worktrees/erk-slot-49/tests/unit/cli/commands/exec/scripts/test_update_pr_description.py` -- 1 occurrence (line 405) 6. `/Users/schrockn/.erk/repos/erk/worktrees/erk-slot-49/tests/unit/cli/commands/exec/scripts/test_setup_impl_from_pr.py` -- 1 occurrence (line 261)

The only `pr_id` near a `save_plan_ref` is in `test_upload_impl_session.py` where `pr_id` is correctly used as a variable value (`plan_id=pr_id`), not a keyword argument name. agentId: aa1eec3ed1c62406a (for resuming to continue this agent's work if needed) <usage>total_tokens: 33937 tool_uses: 28 duration_ms: 104449</usage>

tool_use.name: Agent
tool_use.param: Run `make fast-ci` in /Users/schrockn/.erk/repos/erk/worktrees/erk-slot-49 and report results. Report all failures in detail including exact test names and error messages.

session.tool_result:
## Fast CI Results
**Status: FAILED** with 25 test failures and 8 errors (5957 passed)
### Summary The failures are primarily due to parameter naming changes from `pr_number` to `plan_number` in metadata block creation functions and other plan-related APIs. There are also some assertion failures due to output message changes.
### Detailed Failures
#### TypeError Failures (Parameter Name Changes)
1. **test_create_submission_queued_block() - Parameter renamed** - File: `tests/commands/pr/test_log.py:446` - Error: `create_submission_queued_block()` got unexpected keyword argument `'pr_number'`. Did you mean `'plan_number'`?
2. **test_workflow_started_block() - Parameter renamed** - File: `tests/commands/pr/test_log.py:84` - Error: `create_workflow_started_block()` got unexpected keyword argument `'pr_number'`. Did you mean `'plan_number'`?
3. **test_plan_block() - Parameter renamed** - File: `tests/commands/pr/test_log.py:168` - Error: `create_plan_block()` got unexpected keyword argument `'pr_number'`. Did you mean `'plan_number'`?
4. **test_log_with_all_event_types - Parameter renamed** - File: `tests/commands/pr/test_log.py` (test truncated in output) - Similar parameter renaming issue
5. **test_add_worktree_creation_comment_success - Parameter renamed** - File: `tests/core/test_impl_folder.py:175` - Error: `add_worktree_creation_comment()` got unexpected keyword argument `'pr_number'`. Did you mean `'plan_number'`?
... [45 lines omitted] ...
- Error: Same `add_worktree_creation_comment()` parameter issue #### Metadata Block Creation Errors (8 errors) - Multiple collection initialization errors from block creation functions - Error: `assert 0 == 1` (exit code mismatch) - Error: Output text changed from "No pr numbers provided" to "No plan numbers provided" - Error: `KeyError: 'pr_id'` (workflow inputs changed) - Similar `KeyError: 'pr_id'` failures - Error: `KeyError: 'pr_backend'` (missing workflow input) - Error: Assertion failing - text changed from "Close PR #789" to "Close plan #789" - **Total unit tests (erk)**: 25 failed, 5957 passed, 8 errors
session.assistant: There are more over-aggressive renames where the source still uses `plan_number` or `plan_id`. Let me investigate and fix systematically.

440→ created_at=datetime(2024, 1, 1, tzinfo=UTC), 441→ updated_at=datetime(2024, 1, 2, tzinfo=UTC), 442→ metadata={}, 443→ objective_id=None, 444→ ) 445→ 446→ submission_block = create_submission_queued_block( 447→ queued_at="2024-01-15T12:32:00Z", 448→ submitted_by="testuser", 449→ pr_number=42, 450→ validation_results={"pr_is_open": True}, 451→ expected_workflow="implement-plan", 452→ ) 453→ 454→ comment = render_metadata_block(submission_block) 455→ 456→ issue = _make_issue_info(plan) 457→ fake_issues = FakeGitHubIssues( 458→ issues={42: issue}, 459→ comments={42: [comment]},

240→ 241→ # Invoke WITHOUT issue number argument 242→ result = runner.invoke(cli, ["pr", "dispatch", "--base", "main"], obj=ctx) 243→ 244→ assert "Auto-detected PR #42 from context" in result.output 245→ assert len(fake_gh.triggered_workflows) >= 1, ( 246→ f"Expected workflow trigger, got: {fake_gh.triggered_workflows}\n" 247→ f"Output: {result.output}" 248→ ) 249→ _workflow_name, inputs, _ref = fake_gh.triggered_workflows[0] 250→ assert inputs["pr_id"] == "42" 251→ assert "Traceback" not in result.output 252→ 253→ 254→def test_dispatch_auto_detects_from_impl_context() -> None: 255→ """Test auto-detection from branch-scoped .erk/impl-context/<branch>/ directory.""" 256→ runner = CliRunner() 257→ with erk_isolated_fs_env(runner) as env: 258→ plan_branch = "plnd/auto-detect-context" 259→ pr_42 = _make_pr_42(plan_branch=plan_branch)

535→ plan_store=planned_pr_backend, 536→ ) 537→ 538→ result = runner.invoke(cli, ["pr", "dispatch", "42", "--base", "main"], obj=ctx) 539→ 540→ # Verify: workflow was triggered successfully 541→ assert len(fake_gh.triggered_workflows) >= 1, ( 542→ f"Expected workflow trigger\nOutput: {result.output}" 543→ ) 544→ inputs = fake_gh.triggered_workflows[0][1] 545→ assert inputs["pr_backend"] == "planned_pr" 546→ assert inputs["branch_name"] == plan_branch 547→ 548→ # Verify: create_branch was NOT called (branch was checked out) 549→ assert len(git.created_branches) == 0, ( 550→ f"Expected no create_branch calls, got: {git.created_branches}" 551→ ) 552→ 553→ # Verify: reset_hard was used (not update_local_ref) to keep working tree in sync 554→ reset_calls = git.branch.reset_hard_calls
